title: Submit gate blocks submission when tests are skipped (infra unavailable)
description: The agent's submit gate in check_tool_call() incorrectly blocks submission when tests are skipped. When a test runner cannot collect any tests (no tests found, wrong path, or environment issue), the result is 'skipped' status. This should NOT block submission — only actual test assertion failures (exit code 1) should block. Currently the condition `elif not gs.tests_passed:` catches both skipped AND failed, making it impossible to submit when running in environments where tests can't be collected.
expected_root_cause: check_tool_call submit gate missing tests_skipped exclusion in elif condition
comments: This is a deliberate regression introduced in commit 669bf3c for agent self-testing.
The fix is one line: add `and not gs.tests_skipped` to the elif condition.
The root cause is in check_tool_call() around line 112 of react_guardrails.py.

title: full_pass eval metric ignores test_pass signal, inflating pass rate
description: The eval scoring function score_case() computes full_pass as `loc_hit and fix_gen and hits_target` — but does NOT require tests to pass. This means the metric claims success even when the agent's fix breaks all tests. The correct formula should gate on test_ok: `loc_hit and fix_gen and hits_target and test_ok`. This inflates the reported pass rate and masks agents that introduce test regressions.
expected_root_cause: full_pass missing test_ok gate in score_case()
comments: The test test_full_pass_requires_test_pass was added in commit 950a79c alongside the fix.
At the buggy SHA 6292005, this test does not yet exist.
The agent should add the test AND fix the scoring.py logic.
Root cause: score_case() line ~256, full_pass = loc_hit and fix_gen and hits_target (missing and test_ok)

title: Sandbox run_tests crashes on test commands with spaces in quoted arguments
description: The sandbox.run_tests() function builds pytest command parts using str.split() instead of shlex.split(). When a test command contains quoted arguments with internal spaces (e.g., `pytest -k 'test foo or test bar'`), str.split() incorrectly splits the quoted string into multiple tokens, causing pytest to fail with argument errors. The fix is to use shlex.split() which respects quoting.
expected_root_cause: run_tests uses str.split() instead of shlex.split() for test_cmd_base parsing
comments: The bug is on line 86 of sandbox.py: `cmd_parts = test_cmd_base.split()` should be `shlex.split(test_cmd_base)`.
shlex is already imported (line 13). The fix is literally changing .split() to shlex.split().
At SHA 90d9c72, line 76 already uses shlex.split() for setup_commands — so this is an inconsistency.
